speaker: Jesus
prompt_context: Before the ascension, the risen Lord told the apostles not to leave Jerusalem but to wait for what the Father had promised.
question: Who told the apostles to wait in Jerusalem for the promised Holy Spirit?

speaker: Jesus
prompt_context: Just before ascending, the speaker described the coming power of the Holy Spirit and the spread of witness from Jerusalem outward.
question: Who told the apostles that they would be his witnesses in Jerusalem, Judea, Samaria, and to the ends of the earth?

speaker: Two Men in White Robes
quote_text: Why do you stand looking up?
prompt_context: After Jesus was taken up, two heavenly messengers addressed the disciples as they stared toward heaven.
question: Who asked the disciples why they were standing and looking up into heaven after the ascension?

speaker: Peter
quote_text: One of these must become a witness
prompt_context: Among the gathered believers, someone explained why another man needed to be chosen to join the apostles after Judas.
question: Who said that one of the men who had accompanied the apostles must become a witness to the resurrection?

speaker: Crowd
prompt_context: At Pentecost, people from many nations heard the mighty works of God in their own languages and reacted with amazement.
question: Who asked what the Spirit-empowered speaking at Pentecost meant?

speaker: Peter
prompt_context: At Pentecost, one apostle stood up with the eleven to answer the accusation that the believers were intoxicated.
question: Who told the crowd at Pentecost that the believers were not drunk?

speaker: Peter
prompt_context: At Pentecost, after the crowd was cut to the heart and asked what they should do.
question: Who told the Pentecost crowd to repent and be baptized?

speaker: Peter
quote_text: In the name of Jesus Christ
prompt_context: At the Beautiful Gate, a beggar asked for alms and received a command instead.
question: Who told the lame man at the temple gate to rise and walk in the name of Jesus Christ?

speaker: Peter
prompt_context: Before the rulers and elders, one apostle declared that salvation was found only in Jesus.
question: Who told the council that there was no other name under heaven by which people must be saved?

speaker: Jewish Council
prompt_context: After conferring together, the authorities tried to silence the apostles.
question: Who ordered Peter and John not to speak or teach at all in the name of Jesus?

speaker: Peter
quote_text: Why has Satan filled your heart?
prompt_context: When a man lied about the proceeds of a sale, one apostle exposed the deception publicly.
question: Who asked Ananias why Satan had filled his heart to lie to the Holy Spirit?

speaker: Peter
quote_text: How is it that you agreed together?
prompt_context: A few hours after Ananias died, his wife arrived unaware of what had happened and was confronted about the same lie.
question: Who confronted Sapphira for agreeing to test the Spirit of the Lord?

speaker: Angel of the Lord
quote_text: Go and stand in the temple
prompt_context: After opening the prison doors at night, a heavenly messenger sent the apostles back to public ministry.
question: Who told the apostles to go stand in the temple and speak the words of this life?

speaker: Gamaliel
prompt_context: In the council, a respected Pharisee urged caution by comparing the apostles to failed past movements.
question: Who advised the council to leave the apostles alone in case their work was from God?

speaker: Twelve Apostles
prompt_context: When a complaint arose over the daily distribution, the leading apostles explained their priorities to the believers.
question: Who said it was not right for them to neglect the word of God in order to wait on tables?

speaker: Stephen
prompt_context: In his defense before the council, the speaker turned from Israel's history to rebuking his hearers.
question: Who accused the council of resisting the Holy Spirit in his speech before them?

speaker: Stephen
prompt_context: As he was being stoned, the martyr prayed directly to the exalted Christ.
question: Who asked the Lord Jesus to receive his spirit while being stoned?

speaker: Philip
quote_text: Do you understand what you are reading?
prompt_context: After being sent to the desert road, the evangelist approached an Ethiopian official reading Isaiah aloud.
question: Who asked the Ethiopian official whether he understood what he was reading?

speaker: Ethiopian Official
quote_text: How can I, unless someone guides me?
prompt_context: While reading Isaiah, the traveler admitted he needed help before Philip joined him in the chariot.
question: Who said he could not understand the passage unless someone guided him?

speaker: Jesus
prompt_context: On the road to Damascus, a heavenly voice stopped Saul and identified itself as the one he was opposing.
question: Who spoke to Saul on the Damascus road, asking why he was persecuting him?

speaker: Ananias of Damascus
prompt_context: When told in a vision to visit Saul, a disciple in Damascus voiced concern about Saul's reputation.
question: Who answered the Lord by saying he had heard how much evil Saul had done to the saints?

speaker: Cornelius
prompt_context: At the ninth hour, after seeing an angel in a vision, the centurion responded with fear and reverence.
question: Who asked, "What is it, Lord?" after seeing an angelic vision at Caesarea?

speaker: Voice from Heaven
prompt_context: During Peter's rooftop vision in Joppa, a heavenly voice corrected his refusal to eat what had been shown him.
question: Who told Peter not to call profane what God had made clean?

speaker: Peter
prompt_context: When Peter began speaking in Cornelius's house, he reflected on what he had learned from the vision and invitation.
question: Who declared in Cornelius's house that God shows no partiality?

speaker: Believers from the Circumcision
prompt_context: After Peter returned to Jerusalem, some believers criticized his table fellowship with Gentiles.
question: Who criticized Peter for going in to uncircumcised men and eating with them?

speaker: Agabus
prompt_context: At Antioch, a prophet predicted a widespread crisis by the Spirit.
question: Who foretold by the Spirit that a severe famine would spread over the world?

speaker: Holy Spirit
quote_text: Set apart for me Barnabas and Saul
prompt_context: While the church in Antioch worshiped and fasted, the Holy Spirit gave direction for missionary work.
question: Who said to set apart Barnabas and Saul for the work they had been called to do?

speaker: Paul
prompt_context: In the synagogue at Pisidian Antioch, the missionary concluded his sermon by announcing justification through Jesus.
question: Who proclaimed in Pisidian Antioch that forgiveness of sins was announced through Jesus?

speaker: Crowd in Lystra
prompt_context: After a crippled man was healed in Lystra, the local people misinterpreted what they had seen.
question: Who said that the gods had come down in human form after the miracle at Lystra?

speaker: Certain Believers from Judea
prompt_context: At Antioch, some men arrived teaching that Gentile believers could not be saved without adopting a covenant sign.
question: Who taught in Antioch that salvation required circumcision according to the custom of Moses?

speaker: Peter
quote_text: Why are you putting God to the test?
prompt_context: At the Jerusalem council, one apostle argued against placing a yoke on Gentile believers.
question: Who asked the council why they were testing God by burdening the Gentiles?

speaker: James
prompt_context: After hearing the testimony of Peter, Paul, and Barnabas, a leader in Jerusalem gave the council's concluding proposal.
question: Who said that Gentile believers should not be troubled, except to abstain from a few specific practices?

speaker: Lydia
quote_text: Come and stay at my home
prompt_context: After being baptized in Philippi with her household, a businesswoman urged the missionaries to accept her hospitality.
question: Who insisted that Paul and his companions stay at her home after her baptism in Philippi?

speaker: Slave Girl with a Spirit of Divination
quote_text: These men are servants of the Most High God
prompt_context: In Philippi, a fortune-telling slave girl followed Paul and his companions, shouting a true statement for many days.
question: Who kept crying out that Paul and his companions were servants of the Most High God?
option_c: Slave Girl with a Spirit of Divination
correct_answer: Slave Girl with a Spirit of Divination

speaker: Philippian Jailer
quote_text: What must I do to be saved?
prompt_context: After the prison doors opened and the chains came loose, the jailer fell trembling before Paul and Silas.
question: Who asked Paul and Silas what he must do to be saved?

speaker: Paul
quote_text: I see how extremely religious you are
prompt_context: Standing in the Areopagus, the speaker began by referring to an altar inscribed to an unknown god.
question: Who told the people of Athens that he saw they were very religious in every way?

speaker: Athenians and Foreigners
prompt_context: After hearing Paul's teaching about Jesus and the resurrection, the people at Athens wanted clarification on the strange ideas.
question: Who asked Paul what his unfamiliar teachings meant before he spoke at the Areopagus?

speaker: Town Clerk of Ephesus
prompt_context: After the uproar over Artemis, a civic official calmed the crowd by reminding them of the city's reputation.
question: Who quieted the Ephesian crowd during the riot over Artemis?

speaker: Agabus
prompt_context: At Caesarea, the prophet took Paul's belt and acted out what would happen to its owner in Jerusalem.
question: Who used Paul's belt to predict that its owner would be bound in Jerusalem?

speaker: Paul
prompt_context: While giving his defense in Jerusalem, the speaker retold his encounter with the heavenly voice on the road to Damascus.
question: Who said, "Who are you, Lord?" while recounting the Damascus road experience?

speaker: Ananias the High Priest
prompt_context: When Paul began speaking before the council, the presiding high priest ordered violence against him.
question: Who ordered those standing near Paul to strike him on the mouth?

speaker: Lord
prompt_context: After the turmoil in Jerusalem, the Lord stood near Paul at night and assured him about future testimony.
question: Who told Paul to take courage because he would also testify in Rome?

speaker: Tertullus
prompt_context: Before Felix, an advocate for the Jewish leaders opened the case against Paul with flattery and accusation.
question: Who presented the formal case against Paul before Felix?

speaker: Festus
quote_text: Are you willing to go up to Jerusalem?
prompt_context: As governor, the speaker tried to transfer Paul's case in a way that would please the Jerusalem leaders.
question: Who asked Paul whether he was willing to go to Jerusalem to be tried there?

speaker: Agrippa
prompt_context: Before Paul began his defense before the royal audience, the ruler formally invited him to present his case.
question: Who gave Paul permission to speak in his hearing at Caesarea?

speaker: Festus
quote_text: Paul, you are out of your mind
prompt_context: As Paul spoke about resurrection and the prophets before the royal audience, the governor interrupted loudly.
question: Who interrupted Paul's defense by saying that too much learning had driven him out of his mind?

speaker: Agrippa
quote_text: Are you so quickly persuading me?
prompt_context: After hearing Paul's appeal to the prophets and his own experience, the king answered him directly.
question: Who responded to Paul by asking whether he thought he could persuade him so quickly?

speaker: Paul
quote_text: I perceive that the voyage will be with danger
prompt_context: Before the storm at sea, one prisoner warned those in charge about the risks of continuing the journey.
question: Who warned the ship's company that continuing the voyage would bring danger and loss?

speaker: Paul
prompt_context: During the storm, Paul reported what God's angel had told him in the night concerning survival and standing before Caesar.
question: Who told the terrified sailors and passengers that an angel had assured him he must stand before Caesar?

speaker: Paul
quote_text: This salvation of God has been sent
prompt_context: In Rome, after some local Jewish leaders disagreed over his final appeal from Isaiah, the speaker announced where the message would continue.
question: Who declared in Rome that God's salvation had been sent to the Gentiles and that they would listen?
